kill_fact: No kill_fact: every load-bearing figure independently verifies against primary/IR sources, so the bear (PASS/watch-for-capitulation) thesis SURVIVES the kill-check rather than being broken. The only thing that could not be confirmed is the BULL-side 'third hyperscaler PPA in late innings' — undated, unnamed, no primary announcement exists — which is correctly treated as SOFT and is not load-bearing for the bear case.
corrections: (1) Market cap understated: debate says ~$86B; live FMP quote shows ~$98.4B at the $274.06 spot (share count higher post-Calpine stock consideration). This makes trailing FCF yield even THINNER (~$1.29B / ~$98B = ~1.3%), strengthening, not weakening, the bear. (2) Net debt base ~$5.26B per FY25 10-K (debate said ~$5.9B) — immaterial, slightly conservative. (3) FERC Crane/TMI waiver granted ~2026-06-04 (transfer of 760 MW interconnection rights from retiring Eddystone), not 2026-06-01 — ~3-day date imprecision, immaterial; substance (waiver granted, restart targeted H2-2027, $1B DOE loan Nov-2025) confirms. (4) Crane restart target is H2-2027 (835 MW), not '~2028' as stated in two places in the debate — modest timeline correction, does not change the cash-is-forward-loaded conclusion. (5) Technical context the debate omits: spot $274.06 sits BELOW the 50-DMA (~$284) and 200-DMA (~$319) and is ~34% off the 52-wk high ($412.70) — i.e. the premium-compression / de-rating the bear warns about is ALREADY underway, reinforcing the 'wait for the flush' stance.
evidence: Constellation Energy IR, 'Constellation, Meta Sign 20-Year Deal for Clean, Reliable Nuclear Energy in Illinois' (2025-06-03): 1,121 MW Clinton Clean Energy Center 20-yr PPA, deliveries commence June 2027 — load-bearing PPA #2 CONFIRMED (investors.constellationenergy.com).

Hart Energy / Industrial Info, 'Constellation Completes $16.4B Calpine Deal' (closed 2026-01-07; ~$26.6B total incl ~$12.7B assumed Calpine net debt, ~26 GW gas + Geysers geothermal) — Calpine close CONFIRMED.

Utility Dive, 'Constellation's Three Mile Island nuclear restart gets boost with FERC waiver' (2026-06-04): FERC granted transfer of ~760 MW interconnection rights from retiring Eddystone to Crane; restart targeted H2-2027; backed by $1B DOE loan guarantee (Nov-2025) and 20-yr Microsoft PPA — Crane catalyst CONFIRMED (date corrected to 06-04).

CEG FY2025 results (SEC 8-K / company release): revenue $25.5B (+8%), GAAP net income $2.32B (FELL YoY on MtM/decommissioning), adj EPS $9.39, CFO $4.24B less capex $2.95B = FCF ~$1.29B, net debt ~$5.26B — confirms thin trailing FCF / falling-margin bear core. Live FMP quote 2026-06-18: CEG $274.06, mkt cap ~$98.4B, 50-DMA $284, 200-DMA $319, 52-wk high $412.70 (de-rating already underway).
